cnbc.title: Apple taps John Ternus as CEO to replace Tim Cook, who will become chairman
cnbc.summary: Apple said on Monday that John Ternus is succeeding Tim Cook as CEO, with Cook assuming the role of executive chairman on Sept. 1.

cnbc.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump cabinet, Keith Sonderling takes over
cnbc.summary: Department of Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is resigning from the Trump administration, the White House said.

cnbc.title: Trump threatens Iran again as ceasefire deadline looms, U.S. gears up for peace talks
cnbc.summary: Trump reportedly said Sunday that if Iran doesn't sign a deal, "the whole country is going to get blown up," with its bridges and power plants being targeted.

cnbc.title: Strait of Hormuz ship traffic briefly rose and then slowed after weekend attacks
cnbc.summary: Commercial ships remain cautious about transiting the strait as tensions escalate between the U.S. and Iran again.

cnbc.title: Tariff refunds begin on Monday. These retailers are due big paydays
cnbc.summary: U.S. importers, such as Walmart and Target, could be in line for billions in tariff refunds as the government launches its claims-filing portal on Monday.

cnbc.title: Amazon to invest up to another $25 billion in Anthropic as part of AI infrastructure deal
cnbc.summary: Amazon is boosting its investment in Anthropic, which is committing to spending over $100 billion on Amazon cloud services over the next decade.

cnbc.title: Chair nominee Kevin Warsh says Fed must ‘stay in its lane’ to maintain independence
cnbc.summary: Warsh also expressed firm commitment to fighting inflation with only one mention of the labor market.

cnbc.title: Marvell pops on report it will help Google with custom AI chips. Broadcom shares sink
cnbc.summary: Marvell saw a $2 billion investment from Nvidia in March, as AI demand continues to surge.

cnbc.title: Kash Patel sues The Atlantic for $250 million over alcohol abuse claims
cnbc.summary: The Atlantic article said FBI Director Kash Patel "has alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences."

cnbc.title: Kevin Warsh would be the first tech bro Fed chair. How Silicon Valley shaped him
cnbc.summary: The nominee to lead the Fed is an AI optimist who counts tech titans Peter Thiel and Marc Andreessen among his friends.
